title: Persistence of Unknottedness of Lagrangian Intersections
speaker: Yin Li
abstract: The double bubble plumbing, first studied by Smith and Wemyss, is a Stein neighborhood of two Lagrangian 3-spheres intersecting cleanly along an unknotted circle in some 6-dimensional symplectic manifold. Depending on the identification of the normal bundle of the unknot, there are infinitely many such Stein neighborhoods. We prove that there is no Hamiltonian isotopy of the Lagrangian spheres in any of these Stein neighborhoods so that they become two spheres intersecting along a circle which is knotted in either component, contradicting what happens under smooth isotopies. The proof uses the exact Calabi-Yau structures on the wrapped Fukaya categories to classify spherical Lagrangians in double bubble plumbings. This is joint work with Johan Asplund.

proof_stages.label: Local double-bubble plumbing and rigidity statement
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up the Weinstein local model W_k obtained by plumbing two Lagrangian three-spheres along a clean unknotted circle, explains the integer parameter from the normal-bundle identification/Morse-Bott surgery, and states that no Hamiltonian isotopy supported in this local neighborhood can make the intersection circle knotted.

proof_stages.label: Morse-Bott fibration and wrapped Fukaya computation
proof_stages.summary: The double-bubble plumbing is reinterpreted as a Morse-Bott fibration with fiber T^*T^2 and vanishing cycles a, b, a+kb, allowing a fiberwise-wrapped/localization computation that identifies the wrapped Fukaya category with a Ginsburg DG quiver algebra and, via mirror symmetry, gives finite-dimensional SH_0.

proof_stages.label: Exact Calabi-Yau structures and cyclic quasi-dilations
proof_stages.summary: The speaker introduces exact Calabi-Yau structures as positive-cyclic lifts of smooth Calabi-Yau structures, translates them to cyclic quasi-dilations in equivariant and ordinary symplectic homology, and uses the Ginsburg algebra model to obtain such structures over every field.

proof_stages.label: Exclusion of aspherical Lagrangians
proof_stages.summary: Applying Viterbo restriction to a hypothetical exact K(pi,1) Lagrangian, the cyclic quasi-dilation yields a central unit in a torsion-free group algebra, whose non-torsion behavior contradicts the previously established finite-dimensionality of SH_0.

proof_stages.label: Exclusion of spherical types T, O, and I
proof_stages.summary: To handle homology-sphere ambiguities invisible to Fukaya-category homology, the proof classifies spherical three-manifolds and uses maps to finite quotients A4, S4, and A5, carefully chosen coefficient fields, loop-space marking-map compatibility, and integral group-ring central-unit facts to rule out exact Lagrangians of types T, O, and I.

proof_stages.label: Restrictions on remaining lens and prism cases
proof_stages.summary: The same cyclic-quasi-dilation machinery, now in suitable prime characteristic and using more explicit information about H^0 of the wrapped Fukaya category, shows that possible lens-space or prism-manifold Lagrangians must satisfy prime divisibility constraints tied to the plumbing parameter k.

proof_stages.label: Knot-surgery completion of unknottedness
proof_stages.summary: A hypothetical local Hamiltonian isotopy changing the intersection knot is translated into a Morse-Bott/Dehn surgery problem, and known three-manifold knot-surgery theorems are combined with the preceding Lagrangian embedding obstructions and homology invariance to force the resulting knots to be unknotted.
